generationPolicy.caseSentence: This case trains [phase purpose] through [pattern], using [station task], to prevent [failure mode], while introducing [new surprise].
admissionQuestions: Which pattern does this case belong to?
What exact AMC-style task is being tested?
What clinical or performance failure could occur?
What new surprise makes this case different?
What must the learner be able to do after this case?
Does an existing case already train the same job?
variationSources: recovery after an initially wrong direction
generationPolicy.reserveRule: Open a reserve only when it adds a distinct surprise, addresses a demonstrated weakness or receives explicit approval. Merge or retain it as a reserve when an existing case already trains the same job.
generationPolicy.missingLogicRule: A pattern with missing pattern-specific logic cannot enter generation. Its objective, central capability and core coverage must be deliberately designed and source-reviewed first.
generationPolicy.namePolicy: Use varied, realistic and pronounceable names found in Australian practice. Do not use a name as a cultural stereotype or diagnostic clue, and do not repeat a first name from the previous twenty cases unless continuity is intentional and recorded.

phases.title: Can Kill Fast
phases.purpose: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.

phases.title: Dangerous Mimics
phases.purpose: Compare close alternatives without premature closure.

phases.title: Common Stations
phases.purpose: Build reliable performance in common clinical stations.

phases.title: Hard Conversations
phases.purpose: Communicate risk, uncertainty and difficult decisions safely.

patterns.title: Dangerous Chest Pain
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Distinguish immediately dangerous cardiothoracic causes and enter the correct pathway before certainty.
reserves: normal first ECG with ongoing concern
atypical acute coronary syndrome in diabetes
patterns.expansionRule: Open a reserve only when it adds a distinct recognition or safety decision not already trained by the four legacy cases.

patterns.title: Acute Shortness of Breath
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Recognise respiratory deterioration and choose cause-specific first treatment.
coreCoverage: COPD with oxygen and hypercapnia safety
patterns.expansionRule: The fourth legacy case is justified by a distinct oxygen-safety decision. Further expansion requires a new central decision.

patterns.title: Sudden Neurological Deficit
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Preserve the neurological time anchor while checking reversible mimics and haemorrhage.
patterns.expansionRule: Expand only for a distinct neurological time-critical decision not covered by stroke, mimic or haemorrhage.

patterns.title: Sepsis Pattern
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Recognise organ dysfunction, reassess response and account for high-risk hosts.
patterns.expansionRule: Expand only when a host, source or treatment decision cannot be absorbed into the existing sepsis ladder.

patterns.title: Shock Pattern
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Differentiate concealed blood loss, pump failure and loss of vascular tone before selecting resuscitation.
patterns.expansionRule: Expand only for a shock mechanism or resuscitation decision not trained by the existing ladder or other Phase 1 patterns.

patterns.title: Unstable Gastrointestinal Bleed
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Match physiology and likely source to the appropriate resuscitation and source-control pathway.
reserves: aortoenteric fistula after aortic graft surgery
patterns.expansionRule: Keep generic shock and early-haemoglobin traps inside core cases. Open the reserve only for its distinct catastrophic clue.

patterns.title: Metabolic Collapse
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Identify the controlling metabolic process and apply treatment in the correct sequence.
reserves: mixed diabetic ketoacidosis and hyperosmolar state
patterns.expansionRule: Open a reserve only when its first treatment sequence adds a central decision not trained by the three core pathways.

patterns.title: Cardiac Syncope
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Separate structural risk, fast rhythm and slow rhythm despite recovery between events.
patterns.expansionRule: Keep benign-versus-cardiac differentiation in Pattern 18 and medication-triggered torsades in Pattern 12 unless a dedicated gap is demonstrated.

patterns.title: Acute Severe Abdominal Pain with Instability
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Distinguish rupture, arterial occlusion and contamination while choosing imaging or surgery from physiology.
reserves: strangulated closed-loop obstruction or incarcerated hernia
patterns.expansionRule: Defer stable localisation to later patterns and expand only for a distinct unstable imaging or source-control decision.

patterns.title: Obstetric Catastrophe
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Manage distinct early-pregnancy, late-pregnancy and postpartum emergencies.
patterns.expansionRule: Open a reserve only when its pregnancy-specific immediate action cannot be absorbed into the three core periods.

patterns.title: Paediatric Acute Deterioration
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Recognise paediatric deterioration through circulation, airway or neurological failure.
patterns.expansionRule: Expand only when an age-specific first decision is not already trained by paediatric or parallel adult emergency patterns.

patterns.title: Toxicological Emergency
patterns.objective: Recognise danger and act before diagnostic certainty.
patterns.dominantCapability: Choose the first life-saving action from ventilation, elapsed time, ECG toxicity, heat production or envenoming evidence.
patterns.expansionRule: The five-case legacy expansion is closed. A reserve requires an explicit new central life-saving decision and variation review.

patterns.title: Stable Chest Pain versus Unstable Chest Pain
patterns.objective: Compare close alternatives without premature closure.
patterns.dominantCapability: Classify chest pain from symptom trajectory rather than the label, current pain state or one ECG.
coreCoverage: unchanged exertional pattern requiring outpatient assessment and safety-netting
changed angina pattern requiring reclassification despite absent pain and a normal first ECG
patterns.expansionRule: Expand only for a trajectory decision not trained by the stable and changed two-case ladder.

cases.title: Jaw discomfort while carrying groceries
cases.distinctJob: Recognise possible myocardial ischaemia when exertional jaw discomfort, breathlessness and nausea are volunteered before central heaviness is elicited.

cases.title: Sudden chest and back pain
cases.distinctJob: Recognise acute aortic syndrome from abrupt maximum-at-onset chest-to-back pain plus vascular asymmetry, then explain the immediate safe plan and why empirical aspirin or fibrinolysis is withheld.

nextAction.reason: Cases 1 and 2 have passed reconstruction. Case 3 is the next controlled legacy case.
